description_long: This object is a brown chicken egg that has been cracked open, with two large shell halves separated and a pool of translucent egg white spreading beneath them. The shell is a rich, natural brown color with a smooth, slightly matte finish, and the jagged edges of the break are clearly visible. The interior of the shell is a lighter, off-white shade, contrasting with the darker exterior. The egg white appears glossy and semi-transparent, pooling on the surface below the shell. There are no visible yolk remnants, suggesting the yolk has been removed or is not present. The object is small, delicate, and visually realistic, resembling a freshly cracked egg typically seen in kitchens or on sidewalks after being dropped.
description: A cracked brown chicken egg with two shell halves and a pool of egg white beneath, showing jagged edges and a smooth, matte surface.
description_view_0: The cracked egg is seen from the side, with both shell halves separated and the glossy egg white pooling beneath.
description_view_1: The egg appears whole and unbroken from this angle, with the smooth brown shell facing forward and no visible cracks.
description_view_2: The broken shell halves are clearly visible, with the interior of the shell and the pooled egg white most prominent.
description_view_3: The view highlights the inside of one shell half and the jagged edge, with the egg white visible on the ground.
attribute_orientation: lying on its side with shell halves separated and open edges facing outward
attribute_contextual_placement: Most likely encountered after being cracked open for cooking or accidentally dropped on a hard surface.
